name: Ao Guang
description: Ao Guang, also known as the Dragon King of the East Sea, is a prominent figure in Chinese mythology and one of the Dragon Kings who rule over the Four Seas. He resides in a magnificent crystal palace beneath the ocean, overseeing the marine realm and controlling the weather, particularly rainfall. Ao Guang's power extends to commanding aquatic creatures and influencing the tides. He is often depicted as a powerful and sometimes capricious ruler, capable of both benevolence and wrath. He is a significant figure in Chinese folklore, literature, and religious traditions, embodying the awesome power and unpredictable nature of the sea. His interactions with mortals, particularly through folktales like that of Nezha, highlight the complex relationship between humanity and the divine forces of nature. Ao Guang's role as a weather deity also ties him to agricultural prosperity and societal well-being, making him a figure of both reverence and caution.
subtitle: Dragon King of the East Sea

keyMyths.title: Nezha Conquers the Dragon King
keyMyths.description: In this popular tale, Nezha, a rebellious deity, confronts Ao Guang. Nezha demands tribute from the Dragon King's palace and, when refused, defeats Ao Guang's son and subsequently challenges Ao Guang himself. The conflict escalates, eventually leading to Nezha forcing Ao Guang to submit and promise rain for the people. This myth highlights the themes of rebellion against authority and the power of mortals to influence the divine realm. It is a classic story of divine conflict and the triumph of good over perceived evil.

keyMyths.title: Sun Wukong's Acquisition of the Dragon King's Treasures
keyMyths.description: During Sun Wukong's quest for a suitable weapon, he visits Ao Guang's underwater palace. He demands a powerful weapon and armor. Annoyed, Ao Guang presents him with various magical items, each heavier and more powerful than the last, hoping to deter him. Ultimately, Sun Wukong selects the Ruyi Jingu Bang, a magical staff that can change size at will. This encounter emphasizes the Dragon King's wealth and the Monkey King's audaciousness and insatiable desire for power.

keyMyths.title: Ao Guang's Complaint to the Jade Emperor
keyMyths.description: Following Nezha's disruptive actions, Ao Guang travels to the heavens to plead his case before the Jade Emperor, the supreme ruler of the celestial realm. He seeks retribution for the disrespect and violence inflicted upon him and his family. However, Nezha's father intervenes, preventing Ao Guang from successfully securing punishment for Nezha. This myth illustrates the hierarchical structure of the Chinese pantheon and the complex relationships between different deities.

sources.author: Wu Cheng'en
